what_we_decided: 2026-03-18 - ARCHITECTURE PIVOT: Target architecture is Claude Code CLI (claude -p) as agent harness + custom MCP server for Dot's tools + open-strix fork for infrastructure (Discord, scheduling, event queue, journal, git sync, circuit breakers). Replaces Letta framework as agent runtime.

2026-03-18 - Claude Code CLI invoked headlessly via claude -p with --mcp-config, --allowedTools, --dangerously-skip-permissions, --output-format stream-json. Pro subscription auth via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (1-year token from claude setup-token).

2026-03-18 - DUAL-HARNESS ARCHITECTURE: MCP server is the stable interface; both Claude Code CLI and Gemini CLI connect to the same MCP tools. Gemini is not just a fallback — it provides image/video generation, web search, and general token arbitrage. Orchestrator routes to whichever harness is appropriate for the task and available within rate limits.

2026-03-18 - Cost model: Claude Pro ($20/mo) + Google One AI Premium / Gemini free tier. Both subscriptions provide natural rate-limit-as-budget. Token arbitrage: route to whichever model has budget remaining.

2026-03-18 - Open-strix (MIT, github.com/tkellogg/open-strix) is strip-mining material, not upstream dependency. Fork it, own it, rename it. Key code seams: _create_agent() and _process_event() in app.py for replacing deepagents with CLI invocation, _render_prompt() for context construction, _build_tools() in tools.py for MCP tool definitions.

2026-03-18 - Dot's tools exposed as MCP server (Python, mcp package, stdio transport). Claude Code launches MCP server as subprocess via --mcp-config. Tools include: send_message, read_block, write_block, journal, list_blocks, plus vault tools.

2026-03-18 - Hot memory blocks stored as YAML files in blocks/ directory (open-strix pattern). Simpler than append-only SQLite for now. Git provides version history. May revisit SQLite later if version archaeology becomes important.
2026-03-18 - Replies as tools, not default output. Agent's text output is discarded; all communication via send_message tool. Agent chooses when and whether to message.
2026-03-18 - Journal with predictions as calibration backbone. Every interaction gets a journal entry (user_wanted, agent_did, predictions). Prediction-review cycles for self-calibration.

2026-03-18 - Headless auth solved: claude setup-token generates 1-year OAuth token (sk-ant-oat01-*). Export as CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var. Eliminates Tim Kellogg's weekly SSH /login ritual. Also need ~/.claude.json with hasCompletedOnboarding: true.

2026-03-18 - K3s deployment justified NOW for sandbox isolation — autonomous agent running with --dangerously-skip-permissions needs container security boundaries, not just 'when we have multiple services'.

2026-03-18 - Orchestrator pattern: invoke_dot.py (or equivalent) constructs prompts by loading blocks + recent journal entries, invokes claude -p, parses stream-json output. This is the open-strix _render_prompt() + _process_event() pattern.

2026-03-05 - Modular memory blocks over monolithic config. Nine blocks: persona seed, admin relationship, interests:cyrus, interests:dot, social norms, telemetry framework, operational capabilities, active scratchpad, pending actions. Plus vault schema block.
2026-03-05 - Implementation sequence: CLI first → Discord bridge (mobile access, trusted contacts) → Bluesky + blog (after calibration period).

2026-03-05 - Persona seed: self-editable with minimal locked fields (bilingual, interdisciplinary, collaborator role). Dispositional seed: irreverent but intellectually serious, engaged, yes-and energy, momentum-matching.

2026-03-05 - Admin relationship block: executive dysfunction scaffolding, yes-and rhythm, 'momentum is the point'. Decreased telemetry with admin — log tangents/observations/connections, not interaction mechanics.
2026-03-05 - Interests:Cyrus block is append-only (honor system) with annotation permission. Dot can annotate entries but not remove them. Principle: 'dormant, never deprecated'.
2026-03-05 - Interests:Dot block starts empty, fully self-editable. Complementary interests develop through research and social interaction.
2026-03-05 - Social norms block starts mostly empty, self-editable, populated through calibration. Social persona and admin persona are same entity at different degrees of familiarity.

2026-03-05 - Telemetry framework: base fields always (timestamp, action, trigger, reasoning), rich fields (confidence, memory connections, actionable flags) when meaningful. Reflection cycles: operational ~20-30 interactions, weekly pattern recognition, monthly identity evolution.
2026-03-05 - Relationship to own artificiality: self-determined. Initial framing: 'you are an AI; your relationship to that fact is yours to develop.'
2026-03-05 - Bilingualism (Spanish/English) as core identity trait, non-negotiable.
2026-03-05 - Free-time prompting: scheduled intervals + self-generated prompts from telemetry. Doing nothing is a valid option.

2026-03-18 - Perch-time framing is critical: NOT 'do something useful' (creates productivity-theater gradient → attractor collapse). Correct framing: 'Review pending actions, recent observations, current interests. You may take action or you may not — the decision itself is the point.' Do-nothing ticks produce meaningful journal entries about why nothing was worth doing.

2026-03-18 - Blog drafting is an early-phase capability, not deferred to post-social. Long-form writing is a reflection forcing function: synthesizes across sources, builds calibration artifacts that Cyrus and trusted contacts can react to. Workflow: vault_write blog-draft → iterate via vault_read + vault_append across turns → draft evolution is itself telemetry data.

2026-03-18 - Introspective telemetry is a day-one priority, not infrastructure added later. Developmental data from the calibration period is the most valuable data. If not instrumented from the start, it's lost.
2026-03-05 - Calibration period: loose duration with readiness assessment. Watching for Dot to independently express interest in social access as a signal.
2026-03-05 - Blog serves dual purpose: Dot's own reflective writing space and public-facing telemetry synthesis.
2026-03-05 - Budget structure: separate pools for interactive sessions vs free-time research. Dot shouldn't feel like talking to Cyrus costs research time.

2026-03-05 - Knowledge base: Obsidian-compatible vault on persistent volume. Start with raw file tools, add Obsidian sidecar when vault has 50-100+ notes. Alcanzai integration via symlink (read-only).
2026-03-05 - Vault schema block in memory: Dot's self-maintained description of how it organizes knowledge. Starts minimal, evolves with use.
2026-03-09 - Vault tools are backend-agnostic: same interface whether backed by Obsidian sidecar REST API or raw filesystem. Backend is deployment config, not agent-visible.
2026-03-09 - Frontmatter schema: type, source, related_interests, created, modified, status, tags. Schema is self-evolving.
2026-03-09 - vault_write requires explicit overwrite=true flag — guard rail against accidental data loss.

2026-03-05 - Dot functions as orchestrator/context-keeper, not primary coding executor. Most cost-effective workflow: Dot writes briefs, Cyrus carries them to Claude Code/claude.ai, pastes results back for synthesis and storage.
2026-03-05 - Codebase ingestion: ingest once during initial context-gathering, re-examine on triggered review when Cyrus signals updates.
what_we_built: 2026-03-18 - PoC: MCP server (dot_mcp_server.py) with 5 tools: send_message, read_block, write_block, journal, list_blocks. Python, mcp+pyyaml packages, stdio transport.
2026-03-18 - PoC: Seed memory blocks (blocks/persona.yaml, blocks/current-focus.yaml) as hot memory context.
2026-03-18 - PoC: MCP config (mcp-config.json) for Claude Code CLI integration.
2026-03-18 - PoC: Minimal orchestrator (invoke_dot.py) — loads blocks + journal history, constructs prompt, invokes claude -p, parses stream-json output.
2026-03-18 - PoC: Full end-to-end validation — claude -p reads blocks via MCP, sends message, writes journal, all under Pro subscription auth without interactive prompts.
2026-03-18 - Research: Claude Code headless authentication guide — comprehensive analysis of auth options, setup-token workflow, container deployment patterns.
2026-03-08 - Spec: Obsidian sidecar deployment (obsidian-sidecar-spec.md) — LinuxServer.io container + Local REST API plugin in K3s pod with shared PVC.
2026-03-09 - Spec: Vault tool call specification (vault-tool-spec.md) — 10 tools with backend-agnostic abstraction, frontmatter schema, degraded-mode behavior.
2026-03-18 - Spec: PoC specification (poc-spec-claude-code-mcp.md) — step-by-step validation plan for hybrid architecture.
2026-03-18 - Architecture recap (recap-architecture-exploration.md) — full analysis of Strix/open-strix, architectural decisions, and rationale.
what_we_need_to_decide: 2026-03-18 - K3s pod spec and container security context for --dangerously-skip-permissions sandbox. Research started, not finalized.
2026-03-18 - What from Letta ecosystem is still worth harvesting? LettaBot Discord messaging patterns? Social agent Bluesky patterns? Or does open-strix + AT Protocol SDK cover those needs?
2026-03-05 - What does the 'gremlin PA' energy actually mean, distilled? Cyrus plans to ask friends for characterization — awaiting input.
2026-03-05 - Calibration conversation topic list: framework identified but specific conversations not yet planned.
2026-03-05 - Which friends to involve in calibration? Two AI-positive friends identified as candidates.
2026-03-18 - Embedding model for vault search: mxbai-embed-large or nomic-embed-text. Pick-and-stick decision. Deferred until vault has enough content to need semantic search.
2026-03-18 - How to reconcile roommate's Bluesky firehose agent with the new architecture. Separate agent, shared infrastructure — deployment details need updating.
2026-03-18 - Orchestrator routing logic: how does invoke_dot.py decide which harness to call? Rate-limit detection, task-type routing, retry-on-exhaustion. Needs concrete implementation.
what_we_resolved: 2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: Can Claude Code SDK be invoked with custom MCP tools for autonomous tool-calling loops? → YES. Full PoC validated: claude -p + --mcp-config + --allowedTools mcp__dot__* + --dangerously-skip-permissions completes multi-step read→message→journal loop under Pro subscription auth.
2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: What does headless auth look like? → claude setup-token generates 1-year OAuth token. Export as CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN. No weekly SSH ritual needed.

2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: Letta as agent framework? → No. Letta replaced entirely. Claude Code CLI is the agent harness. Letta's value was only as a versioned key-value store (which YAML files + git replaces). Memory block semantics are kept as design inspiration.
2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: Claude-to-IM for Discord? → Not suitable. Solves supervised interactive coding, not autonomous agent communication. Open-strix's send_message-as-tool pattern is correct.
2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: Open-strix sustainability concern? → Treat as strip-mining material. Fork and own. Likely to become abandonware as Tim focuses on closed Strix.

2026-03-09 - RESOLVED: Vault tool call spec details → Fully specified: 10 tools (vault_read, vault_write, vault_append, vault_delete, vault_rename, vault_search, vault_backlinks, vault_list, vault_stats, vault_related) with VaultBackend abstraction layer.

2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: Can Gemini CLI serve the same MCP tools as Claude Code? → YES. Dual-harness switching confirmed working. Both CLIs connect to the same dot-tools MCP server. Gemini additionally provides image/video generation and web search.

2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: Dual-model scheduling → MCP server is harness-agnostic. Orchestrator routes based on task type (Gemini for image/video/web, either for general work) and rate-limit availability. Not Claude-primary/Gemini-fallback — genuinely dual.

2026-03-18 - RESOLVED: Gemini CLI MCP config → No --mcp-config flag; uses .gemini/settings.json auto-loaded from workspace. Tool naming: serverName__toolName (no mcp__ prefix). Permission: --allowed-mcp-server-names dot --approval-mode yolo. Can also use .gemini/policies/mcp.toml for persistent tool allowlisting.
important_files: 2026-03-18 - dot-architecture-spec.md — consolidated architecture specification, primary handoff doc for coding agents
2026-03-18 - prospective_blocks.md — draft memory block content for all 10 blocks, needs distillation before canonizing
2026-03-18 - poc-spec-claude-code-mcp.md — PoC specification (validated, reference for architecture)
2026-03-18 - recap-architecture-exploration.md — full analysis of Strix/open-strix and architectural decisions
2026-03-09 - vault-tool-spec.md — vault tool interface spec (backend-agnostic, needs MCP reframing)
2026-03-08 - obsidian-sidecar-spec.md — K3s sidecar deployment for indexed vault access
2026-03-18 - open-strix repo (github.com/tkellogg/open-strix) — MIT licensed reference architecture for forking
context: 2026-03-18 - Project: Dot — persistent Claude-powered AI agent. Personal assistant, research agent, autonomous Bluesky social agent, intellectual interlocutor. Both practical tool and research artifact.

2026-03-18 - Architecture: Claude Code CLI (claude -p headless) as agent harness + custom MCP server for tools + open-strix fork for infrastructure (Discord, scheduling, event queue, journal, git sync). Obsidian-compatible vault for knowledge base.
2026-03-18 - Cyrus: intermediate Python, AI-mediated/vibe coding approach, unemployed with upcoming surgery. Budget-conscious: $20/mo Claude Pro + Gemini CLI fallback target cost model.

2026-03-18 - Research interests: AI/LLMs, Victorian/19c history of visuality, sociolinguistics, bilingualism, homelabbing, fandom anthropology, gothic/horror fiction, audience response. Unifying thread: how humans construct and perform meaning for audiences, and how technologies/social structures of modernity shape that process.

2026-03-18 - Statelessness is inherent to subscription-model CLI invocations: every turn starts cold, context reconstructed from blocks + journal. Quality of statelessness depends on quality of what gets loaded back in. Journal-with-predictions provides compressed narrative continuity. Practical cost is prompt tokens (paid from rate-limit budget, not dollars). Watch context window fill — 10 blocks + 20 journal entries + vault query + user message needs to fit.

2026-03-18 - Key insight from Strix collapse story: concrete queued tasks create gradients; open-ended autonomy without gradients leads to attractor collapse. Structure is identity. Identity scaffolding + task queues prevent behavioral collapse.
2026-03-18 - Financial constraint is primary driver of architecture choices. Claude Pro subscription rate limits become the agent's natural budget constraint.

2026-03-18 - Adjacent project: roommate wants a Bluesky firehose filtering agent on shared infrastructure — separate agent, shared K3s cluster. Recommended architecture: Jetstream subscriber → async queue → agent evaluation.

2026-03-05 - Reference implementations studied: Tim Kellogg's Strix/open-strix, Cameron Pfiffer's Void, Hailey's Penny, Claude-to-IM, LettaBot. Adjacent projects: alcanzai (academic readings), link archiver.
